t_ch: 「最后，石头老板被赶跑了，美梦小镇恢复了和平，汽水先生则{TEXTJOIN#90}！」
t_en: "In the end, Boss Stone was driven off and peace was restored to Dreamville, and Mr. Soda {TEXTJOIN#90}!"

t_ch: ——导演，我们这就算定稿了吗？
t_en: ...Director, is that a wrap here?

t_ch: 没错，就是这样的。
t_en: Yes. That's it.

t_ch: 我再考虑一下。
t_en: Let me think about it.

t_ch: 咕噜噜，完成了，谢谢导演！
t_en: *gurgle* All done. Thank you, Director!

t_ch: 这份剧本就给你留做纪念吧——我们的外传一定会很受欢迎的！
t_en: Here's a script for you to keep as a souvenir... Our extra story is going to be a hot property!

t_ch: 咕噜噜，导演，又见面啦！我们的外传十分畅销，多亏了你的帮助！
t_en: *gurgle* Director, there you are! Our extra story is flying off the shelves, and it's all thanks to you!

t_ch: 有兴趣聊聊吗？
t_en: Wanna chat?

t_ch: 好呀，你想要聊点什么？
t_en: Sure. What would you like to talk about?

t_ch: 关于汽水先生。
t_en: About Mr. Soda.

t_ch: 咕噜噜，汽水先生是钟表小子和哈努兄弟的好朋友，他们曾经一起对抗石头老板，维护了美梦小镇的安宁。
t_en: *gurgle* Mr. Soda is a good friend of Clockie and Brother Hanu. They once teamed up to fight against Boss Stone and keep the peace in Dreamville.

t_ch: 他是个贪财又搞笑的家伙，总是为了卖汽水搞出很多乌龙，是美梦小镇中的开心果。
t_en: He's this greedy but funny guy who's always getting up to all kinds of hijinks just to sell his soda. He's the life of the party in Dreamville.

t_ch: 如果传说中的苏萨先生真是这样的人，那可比苏乐达广告里的同谐圣人要有趣多了。
t_en: If the Mr. Sousa of legends really was like that, then he was far more interesting than the saint of Harmony the SoulGlad ads portrayed him as.

t_ch: 关于苏萨先生。
t_en: About Mr. Sousa.

t_ch: 苏萨先生是苏乐达的创始人，据苏乐达企业所说，是他在监狱中发明了美味的苏乐达，用甜美安抚犯人们的痛苦。
t_en: Mr. Sousa was the founder of SoulGlad who, according to SoulGlad Enterprise's statements, invented the refreshing taste of SoulGlad while in prison to sweeten the bitter pain of the prisoners.

t_ch: 但后来他遭到公司迫害，被打入大牢，制作苏乐达的「梦见草」也被灭绝——直到艾迪恩先生来到匹诺康尼，才在梦中重新复原了这种口味。
t_en: But he was persecuted by the IPC and locked up, with the Dreamleaf used for SoulGlad made extinct as well... that is until Mr. Aideen came to Penacony and restored the flavor inside a dream.

t_ch: 这个故事想想就有点不对劲，但真相是什么，估计除了「钟表匠」也没人会知道了……
t_en: There's something not quite right about this story. Whatever the truth is, anyway, I suspect no one will ever get to know besides Watchmaker...

t_ch: 关于「咕噜噜」。
t_en: About the gurgles

t_ch: 咕噜噜是汽水先生的口癖啊，就跟钟表小子的「滴答」和哈努兄弟的「哼」一样，我学的不像吗？
t_en: Gurgling is Mr. Soda's catchphrase, just like Clockie's "Tick-tock" and Brother Hanu's "Hmph." Did I get that right?

t_ch: 这是个人物设定的小技巧，只要有个性的口癖，观众就会对他有更深的印象，甚至成为一种文化符号！
t_en: It's a small trick when designing a character to drop a catchphrase in there. The audience gets a deeper impression of them and it sometimes even can leave a cultural legacy!

t_ch: 下次再聊。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 再见，咕噜噜，祝导演拍摄顺利！
t_en: So long, *gurgle*. All the best with the film shoot, director!

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 再见，咕噜噜，祝导演拍摄顺利！
t_en: So long, *gurgle*. All the best with the film shoot, director!

t_ch: 唉，该死的工作，该死的石头老板！
t_en: Tsk, curse this job and curse Boss Stone!

t_ch: 戴着鳄鱼头套的家伙正在生闷气，没有注意到你的存在…找点能让他平复心情的消息吧。
t_en: The guy in the crocodile hoodie is sulking and doesn't seem to have noticed you... Come up with something to calm him down.

t_ch: 唉，该死的工作，该死的石头老板！
t_en: Tsk, curse this job and curse Boss Stone!

t_ch: 石头老板外传的广告上映了。
t_en: The trailer for Boss Stone's extra story has been released.

t_ch: 天啊，噩耗来得如此突然，克劳克影业不如要了我的命吧！
t_en: No way, such terrible news and so soon. Clock Studios may as well just take my life and be done with me!

t_ch: 如果石头老板是一个强大冷酷的机器人，我也就不用发愁了，那样的反派随便就能吸引一大批粉丝…但他是一只又蠢又坏的肥鳄鱼！连气球周边都卖不出去！
t_en: If Boss Stone were a powerful and clinical robot, I wouldn't be so worried. That kind of bad guy would bring in a stack of fans... But him, he's just a stupid fat crocodile! You can't even sell crocodile balloons as merch!

t_ch: 抛开人气不谈，他的故事我也不知道该怎么写。石头老板是美梦小镇的敌人，也就是匹诺康尼的敌人——但你看看这亲仁善邻的盛会之星，谁会视此处为敌呢？
t_en: Putting aside his popularity for now, I have no idea how to write his story. Boss Stone is the villain of Dreamville and, therefore, is the enemy of Penacony... But just look at this harmonious and friendly Planet of Festivities. Who could ever view this place as the enemy?

t_ch: 是「星际和平公司」！
t_en: The IPC!

t_ch: 哦，很有可能！当年匹诺康尼就是公司手底下的监狱，这和美梦小镇过去是「噩梦小镇」的故事一模一样。
t_en: Oh, it's possible alright! Back then, Penacony was an IPC prison, just as Dreamville used to be known as Nightmareville.

t_ch: 不过现在的公司跟匹诺康尼关系也不差，「钟表匠」先生怎么还惦记着几千年前的旧仇，真是小心眼……
t_en: But the IPC has a good relationship with Penacony now, so I don't know what Mr. Watchmaker is going to worry about a grudge from a thousand years ago. That'd be super petty...

t_ch: 是「家族」！
t_en: The Family!

t_ch: 哎哟，我的老天，大导演，这种话可不敢乱说！
t_en: Oh, my word! Director! Please! Can you not say such crazy things?

t_ch: 不过…咱家老板确实跟家族不怎么对付，说不定还真有可能……妈呀，我真不敢细想了。
t_en: However... our boss really doesn't get on with The Family well at all, so maybe actually... *shivers* I can't bring myself to think seriously about it.

t_ch: 是「星穹列车」！
t_en: The Astral Express!

t_ch: 啊？不能吧，我听说星穹列车上的无名客都是乐善好义的好人，怎么会是无恶不作的石头老板呢？
t_en: Ah? No way, I heard those Nameless aboard the Astral Express are all good folks with hearts of gold. No way they could be someone as unfathomably evil as Boss Stone?

t_ch: 不过听说最近梦里有个喜欢搞事的无名客，招惹猎犬家系、骚扰王下一桶、乱摸皮皮西的绒球——倒也像是石头老板的作风。
t_en: However, I did hear about one Nameless who likes messing around in the Dreamscapes. Getting tangled up with the Bloodhound Family, harassing the Lordly Trashcan, even randomly stroking Pepeshis' fluff balls... That kind of style doesn't seem too dissimilar to Boss Stone.

t_ch: 但不管他是啥，我都对这只鳄鱼一无所知——亲爱的导演，要不你来帮我想点好点子吧！
t_en: But no matter who that person is, I know absolutely nothing about this crocodile — so could you help me riff some good ideas please, Director?

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 天呐，你可真是我的大救星，那就全靠导演了！
t_en: Thank goodness, you're a lifesaver! You can handle the whole thing, then!

t_ch: 我啥也不知道。
t_en: I don't know anything.

t_ch: 哦？那也没事，反正出了问题不用我担责……
t_en: Huh? That's fine, I don't have to shoulder any responsibility anyway if anything goes wrong...

t_ch: 咳，我的意思是——能力越大，责任越大，而且大导演的点子怎么会出问题呢？
t_en: Well, what I mean is... with power comes great responsibility. How could a great director's idea ever go wrong?

t_ch: 不能，拜拜。
t_en: I can't. Bye.

t_ch: 别啊，我的好导演！要是你不帮忙，克劳克影业就得赔上一部外传的预算外加一个好员工了！
t_en: No, my dear director! Without your help, Clock Studios is going to lose the budget for an awesome extra story — not to mention a good employee!

t_ch: 行了，咱也别多说了，马上开始吧——
t_en: Fine, let's say no more and just get started right now...

t_ch: 「嘿，亲爱的观众，这次故事的主角是我们熟知的超级大坏蛋——石头老板！
t_en: "Hey there, viewers. The protagonist of this story is one big bad egg who we're all very familiar with: Boss Stone!"

t_ch: 「很久以前，他奴役着『噩梦小镇』的镇民们，在镇中无恶不作，只为得到……」
t_en: "A long time ago, he subjugated the townsfolk of Nightmareville and did all kinds of wicked deeds — all so he could get..."

t_ch: 宝贵的石头。
t_en: Precious stones.

t_ch: 对对，就是石头。在《钟表小子》的世界观里，石头貌似是很重要的东西，整个美梦小镇是靠石头搭起来的。
t_en: Yes, yes — stones. In the worldview of Clockie, stone seems to be super important. All of Dreamville is built upon stones.

t_ch: 美丽的垃圾。
t_en: Beautiful trash.

t_ch: 啥？他要垃圾干什么…我懂了，你想给反派一点恶心的怪癖，好让观众加深印象，真是高超的创作技巧！
t_en: What? What's he gonna do with trash... I get it, you want to give the villain an odious character trait to create this deep impression on the audience. That really is incredible creativity!

t_ch: 圆满的爱情。
t_en: Wholesome love.

t_ch: 嗯？虽然不知道奴役镇民和获得爱情有啥关系，不过现在观众好像挺喜欢这种套路的——让大反派为爱作恶，似乎就没那么可恨了。
t_en: Huh? I have no idea what subjugating the townsfolk has to do with love, but it's a trope that plays out well with audiences nowadays — getting the villain to do all this evil out of love makes them seem less hateful.

t_ch: 那我们继续——「石头老板在镇中无恶不作，只为得到{TEXTJOIN#91}。但他的行径越来越过分，镇民们压抑的怒火愈发高涨……」
t_en: Let's continue, then: "Boss Stone did all kinds of wicked deeds to the townsfolk, all so he could get {TEXTJOIN#91}. But his behavior grew increasingly excessive, and the stifled rage of the townsfolk began to grow and grow..."

t_ch: 「一天，一颗流星坠落在了小镇中。这场灾难让鳄鱼们手足无措，也给镇民们创造了反抗的机会。
t_en: "One day, a meteor crashed into the town. The crocodiles were at a loss for what to do, creating a chance for the townsfolk to launch a counterattack."

t_ch: 「面对向自己宣战的镇民，石头老板选择了……」
t_en: "Faced with townsfolk openly hostile against him, Boss Stone decided to..."

t_ch: 用暴力镇压。
t_en: Suppress them with violence.

t_ch: 嚯，真是个残忍无情的大坏蛋！
t_en: Ooh, what a brutal and ruthless bad guy!

t_ch: 从中挑拨离间。
t_en: Stir up discord.

t_ch: 嘿，你瞧瞧这诡计多端的鳄鱼。
t_en: Hey, would you just look at this cunning crocodile?

t_ch: 推行民主制度。
t_en: Push for democracy.

t_ch: 啊，石头老板居然会选这么理智的办法吗？
t_en: Huh? Would Boss Stone really ever choose to follow that kind of logic!?

t_ch: 我们继续后面的故事——「面对向自己宣战的镇民，石头老板选择了{TEXTJOIN#92}…但他最后还是失败了，镇民们把他赶出了小镇。」
t_en: Let's continue with our story: "Faced with townsfolk openly hostile against him, Boss Stone decided to {TEXTJOIN#92}... But in the end, he failed. The townsfolk chased him out of town."

t_ch: 「『噩梦小镇』也变成了『美梦小镇』，然而石头老板并没有放弃，他发誓一定要夺回这个原本属于自己的地方。他封锁了小镇的资源，发起激烈的进攻…」
t_en: "Nightmareville soon became Dreamville. However, Boss Stone had not yet given up and vowed to take back this place that once belonged to him. He blocked off the town's resources, and launched a punishing offensive..."

t_ch: 「美梦小镇很快陷入了绝境，镇民们争执不断——还好聪明的钟表小子找来了木头老爷帮忙，在木头老爷的劝说下，镇民重新团结了起来。」
t_en: "Dreamville soon fell into desperation and the townsfolk bickered endlessly — but fortunately, clever Clockie sought out help from Old Man Wood who persuaded the townsfolk to reunite once again."

t_ch: 「石头老板也不敢肆意妄为了，只能……」
t_en: "Boss Stone didn't dare play up anymore. All he could do was..."

t_ch: 暗中给小镇添乱。
t_en: Cause trouble for the town from the shadows.

t_ch: 哦，这只该死的坏鳄鱼，真符合他的作风！
t_en: Huh, that darned crocodile. That is SO like him!

t_ch: 洗心革面，重新做鳄。
t_en: Turn over a new leaf as a new crocodile.

t_ch: 不错，很有教育意义…但之后的故事该怎么拍呢？
t_en: That's good, a nice moral in it... But what about the rest of the story?

t_ch: 和大家幸福地生活在一起。
t_en: Live happily ever after together with everyone.

t_ch: 经典的大团圆结局！不过这转折是不是太随便了……
t_en: The classic happy ending! But isn't that twist a little bit too much of a non-sequitur?

t_ch: 行了，搞定！让我们看看这份外传故事——
t_en: Great, all done! Let's dig into the plot of this extra story then...

t_ch: 「嘿，亲爱的观众，这次故事的主角是我们熟知的超级大坏蛋——石头老板！很久以前，他奴役着『噩梦小镇』的镇民们，在镇中无恶不作，只为得到{TEXTJOIN#91}。」
t_en: "Hey there, viewers. The protagonist of this story is the one big bad egg who we're all very familiar with: Boss Stone! A long time ago, he subjugated the townsfolk of Nightmareville and did all kinds of wicked deeds — all so he could get {TEXTJOIN#91}."

t_ch: 「一天，一颗流星坠落在了小镇中。这场灾难让鳄鱼们手足无措，也给镇民们创造了反抗的机会。面对向自己宣战的镇民，石头老板选择了{TEXTJOIN#92}！」
t_en: "One day, a meteor crashed into the town. The crocodiles were at a loss for what to do, creating a chance for the townsfolk to launch a counterattack. Faced with townsfolk openly hostile against him, Boss Stone decided to {TEXTJOIN#92}!"

t_ch: 「但他最后还是失败了，镇民们把他赶出了小镇，『噩梦小镇』也变成了『美梦小镇』。」
t_en: "But in the end, he failed. The townsfolk chased him out of town, and Nightmareville soon became Dreamville."

t_ch: 「然而石头老板并没有放弃，他发誓一定要夺回这个原本属于自己的地方。他封锁了小镇的资源，发起激烈的进攻…美梦小镇很快陷入了绝境，镇民们争执不断。」
t_en: "However, Boss Stone had not yet given up, and vowed to take back this place that once belonged to him. He blocked off the town's resources, and launched a punishing offensive... Dreamville soon fell into desperation and the townsfolk bickered endlessly."

t_ch: 「还好钟表小子找来了木头老爷帮忙，在木头老爷的劝说下，镇民重新团结了起来。石头老板也不敢肆意妄为了，只能{TEXTJOIN#93}。」
t_en: "But fortunately, clever Clockie sought out help from Old Man Wood who persuaded the townsfolk to reunite once again. Boss Stone didn't dare play up anymore. All he could do was {TEXTJOIN#93}."

t_ch: 「日子一天天过去，镇民们逐渐忘记了石头老板的凶恶，但这个坏蛋一定还在觊觎着美梦小镇。终有一日，他和他的爪牙会再次归来……」
t_en: "As the days passed by, the townsfolk gradually forgot about how brutal Boss Stone was. But still, this bad guy coveted Dreamville for himself. The day would come when he and his lackeys would rise up once again..."

t_ch: ——大导演，咱们的故事这样就讲完了吧？
t_en: ...Director, is that the ending for our story?

t_ch: 没错，就是这样的。
t_en: Yes. That's it.

t_ch: 我再考虑一下。
t_en: Let me think about it.

t_ch: 好吧，让咱换个版本——
t_en: Cool, let's shake it up a little...

t_ch: 太好了，这下外传就能开拍了，苦差事总算有着落了。
t_en: Great, we can start shooting the extra story now that all the tough stuff is out of the way.

t_ch: 导演，你的创意真有意思！跟你聊了一会儿，我居然对这个坏鳄鱼感兴趣了…这份剧本就给你留个纪念吧！
t_en: Director, you really are a fresh creative! Just chatting a little bit with you has piqued my interest in this crocodile... This script really stays with you!

t_ch: 嘿，我的大导演，我的大救星！
t_en: Hey, my director. My savior!

t_ch: 有兴趣聊聊吗？
t_en: Wanna chat?

t_ch: 当然，你帮我解决了一个大麻烦，我肯定随时奉陪。
t_en: Of course, you did me a massive solid for me, so I'm there for you whenever you need me.

t_ch: 关于石头老板。
t_en: About Boss Stone.

t_ch: 哦，该死的石头老板是美梦小镇的敌人，一条邪恶又奸诈的大鳄鱼，《钟表小子》里最大的反派。
t_en: Oh, that blasted Boss Stone is the nemesis of Dreamville. He's this vile and treacherous crocodile who's the main villain in Clockie.

t_ch: 美梦小镇遇到的所有危机几乎都跟石头老板有关，他就是动画里的万恶之源，人人都对他恨之入骨。
t_en: Almost every single disaster in Dreamville was connected to Boss Stone. He's the source of all evil in the series, and everyone loathes him.

t_ch: ——说真的，这样的故事我有点看腻了，一点新意都没有。听说你的《美梦往事》里还有别的反派，我很期待哦。
t_en: ...To be honest, I'm a little over these kinds of stories. There's nothing innovative about it at all. I heard that your Dreamjoy Memoir is using some other villains and I'm really looking forward to that.

t_ch: 关于石头老板的原型。
t_en: About whom Boss Stone is based on.

t_ch: 原型？咱们之前不是聊过吗——石头老板肯定是匹诺康尼以前的敌人，破坏过美梦的大坏蛋。
t_en: Who he's based on? Didn't we already cover this? Boss Stone must have been the old nemesis of Penacony — the big bad guy who'd had his dreams shattered.

t_ch: 听说匹诺康尼在阿斯德纳星系存在了上千年，肯定遇到过各种危险…但家族把大家保护得这么好，谁还会天天惦记那些破事。
t_en: I heard Penacony has existed in the Asdana System for millennia. It must have faced all manner of disasters... But then again, The Family has kept everyone perfectly safe, so who would be worrying about such things all the time?

t_ch: 要我说啊，历史上可能根本不存在石头老板，只是钟表小子的故事里必须有一个反派而已。
t_en: If you ask me, there might not have ever been a Boss Stone in history — the Clockie stories just needed a villain, that's all.

t_ch: 关于「石头」。
t_en: About "stones."

t_ch: 在钟表小子的故事里，石头是鳄鱼们的最爱——美梦小镇里有很多漂亮的宝石，这也是石头老板不放过小镇的原因。
t_en: In the Clockie stories, stones are what the crocodiles love most — Dreamville has lots of beautiful stones, and this is also why Boss Stone has his sights set on it.

t_ch: 当初的镇民们天天给他挖石头，真是太可怕了！虽然做动画很苦，但肯定比挖石头要舒服多了…吧？
t_en: Back then, the townsfolk would dig up stones for him all day. It was so terrible! Animating is seriously hard work, but it's far more comfortable than digging up stones... right?

t_ch: 下次再聊。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 再见，好导演，祝你在生活中不会遇到石头老板这样的坏蛋。
t_en: So long, maestro! I hope you'll never encounter for yourself a bad guy like Boss Stone.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 再见，好导演，祝你在生活中不会遇到石头老板这样的坏蛋。
t_en: So long, maestro! I hope you'll never encounter for yourself a bad guy like Boss Stone.

t_en: So what? Hmph, my work now is done!
...Hmph.

t_en: Pisce asked me to find you and make the extra stories.

Hmph...

t_en: Hmph.

t_ch: 导演，真不是我怠慢这份工作，对于哈努兄弟的故事，一直有些问题困扰着我——你说，哈努兄弟到底是什么样的人呢？
t_en: Director, it's not that I look down on this job, it's just that there are a few things about the Brother Hanu story that still bug me. Tell me, what kind of person is Brother Hanu exactly?

t_ch: 哼，即便是对历史毫无兴趣的观众，也都应该听说过哈努兄弟的原型——解放匹诺康尼的{RUBY_B#反抗者}哈努努{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Hmph, even people who know nothing about history should still have heard of who Brother Hanu was modeled after — {RUBY_B#Rebel}Hanunue{RUBY_E#}, the one who liberated Penacony.

t_ch: 是他将监狱从公司的奴役中解放出来，赋予此处「匹诺康尼」之名…但匹诺康尼似乎从不感恩这位英雄，我在梦里从没见过他的塑像或颂歌。
t_en: He was the one who broke the IPC's hold over the prison and named it "Penacony"... Yet Penacony has never seemed to thank this hero, and I don't recall ever seeing his statue or hearing his theme song in the dreams.

t_ch: 或许他根本不存在。
t_en: Maybe he never existed.

t_ch: 哼，说不准——除了那些到处寻找创作素材的导演与作家，普通人从不在意匹诺康尼的过去，搞不好这个名字只是哪部电影里的虚构角色而已。
t_en: Well, I don't know... Everyday people don't care about Penacony's past, except for directors and artists constantly mining everything for new material. Maybe the name is actually just a fictional one borrowed from some movie.

t_ch: 或许是有人隐瞒了他的故事。
t_en: Maybe someone out there's hiding his story?

t_ch: 哼，很有可能——解放匹诺康尼这样伟大的事业竟是由一个罪犯完成的，没法抢功的家族肯定想让他的名字从梦中消失。
t_en: Hmph, it's more than likely — the mighty liberation of Penacony was carried out by a lowly crook... If they couldn't steal his thunder, you can bet The Family would want his name struck from the Dreamscapes.

t_ch: 哼。
t_en: Hmph.

t_ch: 喂，这么捉弄人可一点都不酷！
t_en: Hey, don't make fun of me. That ain't cool!

t_ch: 不过要创作「哈努兄弟」的故事，就必须对这个神秘的家伙有所了解。
t_en: But if you want to tell a tale about Brother Hanu, you'll have to understand a little more about this mysterious character.

t_ch: 为了搞清楚哈努努到底是何许人也，我收集了很久的信息，但却调查出了三个截然不同的说法！
t_en: I spent a long time gathering up info to find out what kind of person Hanunue actually was, but I somehow turned up three different stories!

t_ch: 哪三个说法？
t_en: What were they?

t_ch: 第一个形象是我在「匹诺康尼大博物馆」找到的，展馆的看板上说，哈努努是一个<color=#dbc291ff>凶狠无情的罪犯</color>，曾犯下过滔天大罪。
t_en: The first story I found was from a Penacony Grand Museum, where an information board claimed Hanunue was a <color=#dbc291ff>merciless criminal</color> who had committed a flurry of serious felonies.

t_ch: 他入狱前在蒙托尔星系制造了一场战争，令上千万条无辜的生命葬身于战火。虽然凭着粗暴的手段解放了匹诺康尼，也因为粗暴的统治而将匹诺康尼变为了噩梦。
t_en: He waged war in the Montour System prior to his imprisonment that cost millions of innocent lives, and despite freeing Penacony using brutal means, it was those same brutal means that turned Penacony into a nightmare.

t_ch: 暴政下被处刑的尸体堆积成山，灾害与战争更是不计其数…博物馆中的许多资料都记录了那时的惨状，家族应该也没必要在这方面造假。
t_en: The executed corpses piled up high as mountains under his cruel regime, with war and disaster ravaging the world... Lots of material in the museum recounted horrors of the time, and there's probably no reason for The Family to have faked any of that.

t_ch: 第二个呢？
t_en: What was the second one?

t_ch: 第二个形象是我从折纸大学的一位历史教授口中听来的故事，他告诉我，哈努努是一个<color=#dbc291ff>正直无私的勇者</color>。
t_en: The second story I heard was from a history professor at Paperfold University who told me Hanunue was a <color=#dbc291ff>righteous and selfless hero</color>.

t_ch: 据那位教授所说，哈努努是因奸人陷害而蒙冤入狱。他是一位善良勇敢的哈贝克狼人，不忍目睹狱友们蒙受苦难，带领大家揭竿而起，反抗公司的暴政。
t_en: According to this professor, Hanunue had been set up and wrongfully imprisoned. He was a kind and courageous Harbeck werewolf who could not bear to see his fellow inmates suffering — and so he led them in an uprising against the tyranny of the IPC.

t_ch: 而后战争胜利，作为最大功臣的他选择让渡权力，成立家系制度，作为「猎犬」之主捍卫此处的自由，直至逝世前夜仍在荒芜的梦中为匹诺康尼而战。
t_en: After winning the battle, he chose to cede power as the biggest hero and established the system of different lineages. He defended this world's freedom as the leader of the Hounds and fought for Penacony in the barren Dreamscapes right up until the eve of his death.

t_ch: 第三个呢？
t_en: What about the third one?

t_ch: 至于第三个形象，可能有点离谱…这是我在「初醒图书馆」看到的野史——哈努努只是一条普通的<color=#dbc291ff>阿斯德纳野狼</color>。
t_en: As for the third story, it might sound a little outrageous... but it's some unofficial history that I read about in the Primal Waking Library: Hanunue was just an ordinary <color=#dbc291ff>Asdana wild wolf</color>.

t_ch: 在当年的边陲监狱，犯人们的怒火压抑到了顶点，但没人敢打响反抗的第一枪…直到一只失控的狼闯进监狱，咬伤了公司的安保，直接引发了囚犯哗变。
t_en: At the frontier prison, suppression of inmates had reached a zenith. However, nobody dared to fire the first shot... until an out-of-control wild wolf stormed into the prison and bit an IPC guard, triggering mutiny among the inmates.

t_ch: 独立后的犯人们以这只野狼为原型，杜撰出了一位匹诺康尼的独立英雄「哈努努」，但他其实并不存在，只是一个浪漫的传说……
t_en: Following independence, the prisoners used this wolf to build up the hero of Penacony's independence: Hanunue. But he never actually existed — he was just a romantic legend...

t_ch: 导演觉得「罪犯」、「勇者」、「野狼」这三个形象谁才是真正的「哈努努」呢？好好想想一下吧，我将根据你的回答来创作哈努兄弟的故事。
t_en: Which of these three prototypes — "criminal," "hero," or "wolf" — do you think was the real Hanunue? Think carefully about it. I'll write a Brother Hanu story based on your answer.

t_ch: 凶狠无情的罪犯。
t_en: A merciless criminal.

t_ch: 哼，我明白了，那哈努兄弟的背后肯定也有不少黑暗的罪行吧。
t_en: Hmph, got it. There must be a string of dark crimes lurking in Brother Hanu's past.

t_ch: 正直无私的勇者。
t_en: A righteous, selfless hero.

t_ch: 哼，我明白了，那哈努兄弟的墨镜下一定有一双温柔的眼睛吧。
t_en: Hmph, got it. Brother Hanu's definitely got a pair of kind eyes beneath those cool sunglasses.

t_ch: 阿斯德纳野狼。
t_en: An Asdana wolf.

t_ch: 哼，我明白了，那哈努兄弟应该也是一个背负美好愿景的谎言吧。
t_en: Hmph, got it. Brother Hanu is a fabrication representing a beautiful hope.

t_ch: 感觉都不太对…
t_en: None of them feels super accurate...

t_ch: 哼，那又怎样？咱们拍的是动画，不是历史纪录片——你挑个自己喜欢的方案就好了，真假又不影响票房！
t_en: Hmph, so what? We're making an animation, not shooting a historical documentary — so just pick your favorite one. Whether it's true or false won't impact ticket sales!

t_ch: 好，那我就按照你选择的方案创作哈努兄弟的外传了！
t_en: Alright then, I'll write this Brother Hanu extra story based on the idea you picked!

t_en: I'll leave the script in your good hands, then, director. Keep exploring for more Brother Hanu stories inside Dreamjoy Memoir.

t_en: Hmph, Brother Hanu was the top brass around Dreamville, leading the townsfolk to rise against Boss Stone before Clockie's Compass arrived in town.

t_ch: 他是钟表小子最信赖的好伙伴，靠着锐利的目光与手中的火箭炮，数次拯救美梦小镇于水火之中。
t_en: He's Clockie's most trustworthy partner, having saved Dreamville with his sharp eyesight and rocket launcher from disaster multiple times.

t_ch: 如果说钟表小子是美梦小镇的大明星，木头老爷是美梦小镇的大靠山，他就是美梦小镇的大英雄！
t_en: If Clockie is Dreamville's star celeb and Old Man Wood is its big backer, then Brother Hanu's gotta be Dreamville's big hero!

t_ch: 关于哈努努。
t_en: About Hanunue.

t_ch: 哼，虽然我调查了很久，但也不明白哈努努到底是什么人。
t_en: Hmph, I've been investigating this for a long time, but I still don't know who Hanunue actually is.

t_ch: 即使他真的存在，也已经是上千年前的历史人物了——「钟表匠」先生真的会认识他吗？
t_en: Even if he really does exist, he's a historical figure that must've been thousands of years old — would Mr. Watchmaker really know him?

t_en: About *hmph*.

t_en: Director, thank you for telling me all that. Their stories should not be forgotten... I'm going to use this extra story to record their biographies!

t_en: Tweet! It's very possible. In the animation, they built Dreamville using stones and branches — just like how the Dreamweavers built Penacony using memoria!

t_ch: 关于折纸小鸟。
t_en: About the Origami Birds.

t_ch: 啾，折纸小鸟是美梦小镇的建造者，动画中的每个镜头几乎都能看到它们的身影。
t_en: Tweet, the origami birds are the creators of Dreamville, and can be seen in almost every single shot of animation.

t_ch: 它们勤劳又聪明，在猫头鹰老师与五位头领的指挥下，创造了许多神奇的建筑——就和梦境一样！
t_en: Hard-working and clever, they have created many magical structures under the watchful guidance of Professor Owl and their five leaders — just like the dreamscapes!

t_ch: 关于折纸小鸟的头领。

t_ch: 啾，折纸小鸟的头领有五个——妃色夫人、草绿经理、赤红大哥、黄金公子，还有蓝色的湛蓝爵士！它们性格不同，也建造了不同的作品！
t_en: Tweet, there are five leaders of the Origami Birds: Lady Vermilion, Verdant Manager, Boss Red, Young Master Gold, and Dr. Blues! They all have different personalities and make different creations!

t_ch: 妃色夫人设计了华丽的宾馆，草绿经理修建了木头老爷的家宅，赤红大哥建造了汽水工厂，黄金公子送给了钟表小子一座巨大的游乐园……
t_en: Lady Vermilion designed a luxurious hotel, Verdant Manager built Old Man Wood's mansion, Boss Red constructed the soda factory, and Young Master Gold gifted Clockie an enormous amusement park...

t_ch: 不过最神秘的是湛蓝爵士，动画里从来没提到过它建造的作品…不知道《美梦往事》里有没有关于他的故事呢？
t_en: But most enigmatic of all is Dr. Blues, who is never shown building anything at all in the animation... I wonder if there's any stories about him in Dreamjoy Memoir?

t_en: I think so, too.

t_ch: 是吧，这背后的原因可就复杂了……
t_en: Right? The reasoning behind this is really complicated...

t_ch: 因为木头老爷太丑了吧。
t_en: Because Old Man Wood is very ugly.

t_ch: 咳，比起其他的角色，他确实有点不可爱……
t_en: *cough* Compared to other characters, he's not very cute...

t_ch: 木头老爷是啥？
t_en: What's Old Man Wood?

t_ch: 咳咳！你看看！导演都不知道这个角色！
t_en: *cough* Golly! The director doesn't even know about the character!

t_ch: 木头老爷原本是《钟表小子》动画第一集就出现的角色，那时的它是一个德高望重的老者，与钟表小子亦师亦友，是镇民们信赖的大家长。
t_en: Old Man Wood was originally a character from the very first episode of Clockie. Back then, he was this upright old citizen, a teacher and a friend to Clockie, and very trusted among the townsfolk.

t_ch: 但随着剧集更新，这个角色逐渐发生了转变，从一个温和聪颖的智者转变为了一个专横的老顽固。他的藤蔓缠住了每个镇民的房子，钟表小子也开始反抗他的教导。
t_en: But as the series progressed, this character gradually shifted from a gentle and wise old man into this arrogant old fogey. His vines start wrapping around the townhouses, and Clockie starts to push back against his teachings.

t_ch: 或许是家族察觉到了什么，在一次约谈过后，这个角色几乎就没有在剧集中登场过了……
t_en: Perhaps The Family picked up on something, because after one meeting, this character never made it back onto the series again...

t_ch: 跟那些天真的年轻人不同——老朽很明白，《钟表小子》讲述的正是匹诺康尼过去的故事，这样的变化一定也有着自己的深意。
t_en: Unlike naive young people, I understand that Clockie is a story about Penacony's past, and that this change must mean something in and of itself.

t_ch: 但至于这样的变化代表了什么，我始终没有想明白…导演，你怎么看呢？你的想法将会成为老朽创作的启示——
t_en: But as for what such change represents, I still don't know... Director, what do you think? Your ideas will form the basis of this old codger's inspiration...

t_ch: 家族的权力在扩张。
t_en: The Family's power grows ever more encompassing.

t_ch: 呵呵，这还真是个危险的主题，但这的确是客观事实。从最初的援助者到如今的统治者，无论是好是坏，匹诺康尼已经被他们改变了太多。
t_en: Ha, well that's a touchy theme, but it sure is the truth of it. From the initial aid providers to the current rulers, Penacony has changed an awful lot — for better or for worse.

t_ch: 如果是这个主题，那应该就是木头老爷逐渐被权欲吞噬，走向了偏执的道路……
t_en: If that's the theme, then Old Man Wood would gradually be swallowed up by a ravenous lust for power, spiraling toward a path of paranoia...

t_ch: 美梦失去了自由。
t_en: The dreams lost their freedom.

t_ch: 呵呵，不容易，很多年轻人都没有发现这样的变化。在他们的心中，这里一直都是自由的乐园。
t_en: Ha, that's interesting. Lots of youngsters have failed to catch onto this change. In their eyes, this place has always represented a free utopia.

t_ch: 如果是这个主题，那应该就是木头老爷厌恶小镇的散漫，开始采取极端的手段……
t_en: If that's the theme, then it should be about Old Man Wood hating the loose disorganization of the town and the startling countermeasures he starts to take...

t_ch: 美梦得到了秩序。
t_en: Sweet dreams became orderly.

t_ch: 秩序…是啊，橡木家系为美梦增添了诸多律令，才能让这场美梦维系下去。但「钟表匠」先生是个无拘无束的人，任性的他一定不理解家族的苦心。
t_en: Order... Yes, the Oak Family had added many rules to try and keep the dream alive. But Mr. Watchmaker is an unrestrained man, and someone as stubborn as he is wouldn't understand The Family's hard work.

t_ch: 如果是这个主题，那应该就是木头老爷希望让小镇变得更加和平，但是遭到了钟表小子的误会……
t_en: If that's the theme, then Old Man Wood should be trying to bring peace to the town — but ends up getting misunderstood by Clockie...

t_ch: 我的祖父曾跟我讲过，过去的匹诺康尼简直是一团糟。任何恶徒都可以来到这片幻梦之中，梦里梦外纷争不断，甚至朝露公馆都曾被暴徒攻陷……
t_en: My grandpa once told me that the old Penacony used to be a mess. Any bad egg could come into this land of fantasy, and there was constant conflict inside and outside the dreams. Even the Dewlight Pavilion was once overrun by crooks...

t_ch: 直到家族改革，出现了五大家系之上的「梦主」。那位神秘的梦主，改变了自由散漫的匹诺康尼，让家族的目光遍布整片梦境，混乱的梦境才回到正轨。
t_en: It stayed that way until The Family conducted their reforms and created the Dreammaster, who ruled above the Five Lineages. The mysterious Dreammaster changed free and lax Penacony, letting The Family's gaze cover all of the Dreamscapes until those chaotic dreams were back to normal.

t_ch: 但你也看到了，在这个过程中，家族不断伸展的枝蔓也会阻挡渴望奔跑的人们，被它绊倒的不只是钟表匠与克劳克影业…这是好事还是坏事呢？
t_en: But, as you can see, the ever-extending tendrils of The Family would stop those who deserve to run during this process. It wasn't just the Watchmaker and Clock Studios who got entangled and fell... Was this good news or bad news?

t_ch: 呵呵，我说的太多了。总而言之，我会按您的猜想去创作这份外传，请稍等……
t_en: Ha, I've said too much. In any case, I'll write this extra story based on your suppositions. Hold on a second...

t_ch: 木头动画师开始撰写木头老爷的故事……
t_en: The Wood Animator begins scribbling some stories about Old Man Wood...

t_ch: 咳咳，好了，这份故事就交给你吧。
t_en: *cough* Alright, I'll leave this story in your hands.

t_ch: 至于刚才的问题…相信导演心中会有自己的答案吧。
t_en: As for that question... I believe you'll have your own answer, deep down in your heart.

t_ch: 咳咳，怎么了，导演？
t_en: *cough* What's up, director?

t_ch: 有兴趣聊聊吗？
t_en: Wanna chat?

t_ch: 呵呵，请便吧。
t_en: Ha, be my guest.

t_ch: 关于木头老爷。
t_en: About Old Man Wood.

t_ch: 木头老爷呀…它是美梦小镇的一棵大树，帮助小镇摆脱了毁灭的危机，还为小镇开拓了辽阔的土地。
t_en: Old Man Wood...? It was a massive tree in Dreamville that helped the town shake off perilous times of uncertainty and develop vast swathes of terrain for expansion.

t_ch: 最开始的时候，他像是钟表小子的长辈，总是为他讲述各种道理，让幼稚的钟表小子逐渐长大……
t_en: At the very start, he was like a mentor to Clockie — always explaining all kinds of logic to him so that this juvenile little Clockie boy could gradually grow up...

t_ch: 但到了后来，一场火烧掉了他的树冠，一场火烧空了他的枝干…他变得顽固不化，冷漠刻薄，犹如一株枯木……
t_en: But later on, a fire burned his tree canopy away and all his branches off... He became bitter, stubborn, cold, and mean like a withered old dead tree...

t_ch: 关于梦主。
t_en: About the Dreammaster.

t_ch: 咳咳，我并未亲眼见过梦主，只是在电视节目与家族的宣传中了解过他。据说在匹诺康尼即将毁灭的时刻，是他带领家族到访，挽救了这场即将毁灭的噩梦。
t_en: Ahem, I haven't met the Dreammaster in person before. I only know him from TV shows and The Family's promotions. They say that when Penacony was facing destruction, it was him who led The Family here and saved that nightmare situation from impending doom.

t_ch: 他仁慈、和蔼、睿智，是家徒们的导师与偶像。即便在恶徒的迫害中饱受痛苦，也从未放下「同谐」的博爱——这就是我们的梦主，或是说「我们能看到的梦主」。
t_en: He was kind, affable, wise, and a teacher and idol to his disciples. Even though he suffered great hardships at the hands of evildoers, he never abandoned his love of Harmony — this is our Dreammaster... or at least the Dreammaster we can see.

t_ch: 下次再聊。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 呵呵，好呀，我很愿意跟年轻的动画人聊天。
t_en: Ha, sure. I love chewing the fat with young animators.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 呵呵，好呀，我很愿意跟年轻的动画人聊天。
t_en: Ha, sure. I love chewing the fat with young animators.

t_ch: 哎呀，急急急急急！急死了！别吵我！
t_en: Ahhh, move! Move move move! This is urgent! Be quiet!

t_ch: 这个仓鼠小子似乎完全不想听你讲话，得想办法让他老实点……
t_en: This hamster punk doesn't seem interested in listening to what you have to say. You have to find a way to make him behave a little better...

t_ch: 哎呀，急急急急急！急死了！别吵我！
t_en: Ahhh, move! Move move move! This is urgent! Be quiet!

t_ch: 仓鼠球骑士外传的广告上映了。
t_en: The trailer for Hamster Ball Knight's extra story has been released.

t_ch: 啊！怎么这么快！比仓鼠球骑士的「音速坚果」还快！
t_en: Ah, that was fast! Faster than the Hamster Ball Knight's Velocinut!

t_ch: 等等！你等等！导演，你快帮我想想办法！
t_en: Wait! Wait up! Director, please can you help me come up with something?

t_ch: 怎么了？
t_en: Something the matter?

t_ch: 你先别急。
t_en: Hold your horses.

t_ch: 跟那些奇怪的卡通形象不一样，仓鼠球骑士并不是虚构的形象，而是确实存在的人物！他上周刚在「流星球笼竞速赛」中夺得了冠军！
t_en: Hamster Ball Knight is different from the rest of those cartoon weirdos because he isn't imaginary — he's real! Just last week, he came first in the Meteor Spheroid Race!

t_ch: 但反倒给我带来了麻烦——作为鸢尾花家系旗下的球笼明星，我完全申请不到采访他的机会，要不是他给我们的形象授权没过期，这部外传肯定都不让我拍！
t_en: But it actually brought me trouble — as a Spheroid star under the Iris Family, I can't even apply for an interview with him. If it weren't for the fact that our authorization for his likeness is still valid, I wouldn't even be allowed to make this extra story!

t_ch: 按照《钟表小子》过去的故事，仓鼠球骑士是个除了球笼什么也不在乎的大笨蛋，总是因为比赛让自己陷入险境之中……
t_en: Based on old Clockie lore, Hamster Ball Knight was this big doofus who didn't care about anything except Spheroids. He was always putting himself into dangerous situations in the name of a race...

t_ch: 但他背后的故事一定没有这么简单，他一定是为了更重要的东西才没命参加比赛的！但我完全不知道他是为了什么！
t_en: But his background story can't just be that simple. He has to be competing for the sake of something more important to continue to risk his life! It's just I have no idea what that should be!

t_ch: 导演！你觉得仓鼠球骑士是为了什么才这么卖命的？快快快，快想想！我听你的！
t_en: Director! What do you think Hamster Ball Knight was risking his life for? Quick quick quick, come up with something! I trust you!

t_ch: 钱！
t_en: Money!

t_ch: 啊哈，这个合理！那家伙收集了一大堆金灿灿的坚果，肯定是个贪心的仓鼠！
t_en: Aha, now that adds up! That guy had a huge collection of hard, shiny nuts. He must be a greedy stockpiling hamster!

t_ch: 他人的爱。
t_en: The affection of others.

t_ch: 嘿，冠军的确能得到很多人的喜爱，哪怕是个胖乎乎的仓鼠，也能通过胜利收获无数粉丝的青睐…看来仓鼠球骑士也是只缺爱的鼠呀。
t_en: Hey, a champion certainly does receive affection from plenty of others. Even a chubby hamster can earn the adoration of countless fans by just winning... Seems like all Hamster Ball Knight needs is a little love.

t_ch: 比赛，爽。
t_en: The joy of the race.

t_ch: 呀，这个够直接！骑着球笼奔驰确实是个很爽的事情，我也喜欢跑到热砂飙球…在驾照被猎犬吊销之前。
t_en: Jeez, that's straight to the point. Racing around in that Spheroid really is all about joy, and I used to love Spheroid racing in Scorchsand, too... until the Bloodhounds eventually revoked my license.

t_ch: 好！这下我懂了，我就按你的说法开始创作了，别反悔哦——冲冲冲冲冲！
t_en: Great! I get it now. I'll start pulling some things together based on what you said, so there's no going back now... Go, go, go!

t_ch: 仓鼠动画师开始撰写仓鼠球骑士的故事……
t_en: The Hamster Animator starts scribbling some stories about Hamster Ball Knight...

t_ch: 搞定！我真快！
t_en: Donezo! Dang, I'm fast!

t_ch: 喏，这份故事也给导演看看吧，不用谢！
t_en: Hey, this copy of the script is for you, director. You're welcome!

t_ch: 怎么了怎么了？有话快说！
t_en: What's up? What's wrong? Quickly, say something!

t_ch: 有兴趣聊聊吗？
t_en: Wanna chat?

t_ch: 聊！都可以聊！快点聊！
t_en: Chat! Everyone can chat! Chat now!

t_ch: 关于仓鼠球骑士。
t_en: About Hamster Ball Knight.

t_ch: 仓鼠球骑士是美梦小镇的球笼明星！美梦小镇的镇民最喜欢看的就是球笼比赛——这点和现实的梦境没什么区别！
t_en: Hamster Ball Knight is the Spheroid-racing celebrity of Dreamville! The townsfolk love watching Spheroid races — it's not that different from real life!

t_ch: 仓鼠球骑士每次都能在球笼比赛中得到第一名，并通过比赛挫败坏人的阴谋，就连钟表小子都是他的粉丝！
t_en: Hamster Ball Knight always places first in Spheroid races while thwarting the bad guy's scheme throughout the race. Even Clockie is one of his fans!

t_ch: 关于现实中的仓鼠球骑士。
t_en: About the real Hamster Ball Knight.

t_ch: 现实中的仓鼠球骑士叫做「哈姆兹」，作为啮齿目的智慧生命，它和动画里一样可爱，也一样*快*！几千场比赛未尝一败！
t_en: The real Hamster Ball Knight is named "Hamz." As an intelligent rodent, it's just as cute and just as fast as in the animation! That's thousands of races without a single loss!

t_ch: 当年「钟表匠」先生就超级喜欢看他的比赛，才把他加进了动画里——简直就是公费追星嘛！
t_en: Back then, Mr. Watchmaker used to love watching those races so much that he just added him into the animation — it's basically using the company's money to support your idol!

t_ch: 但他已经很久没有接受过采访了，除了比赛之外，人们也没有见过它…真是奇怪！
t_en: But the hamster hasn't been interviewed in a very long time, and nobody has seen him outside of any races... How weird!

t_ch: 下次再聊。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 拜拜！回球见！
t_en: Bye! See you again soon!

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 拜拜，回球见！
t_en: Bye! See you again soon!

t_ch: 不如让家族收购了。
t_en: Might as well let The Family buy it.

t_ch: 反正钟表匠也已经…
t_en: In any case, the Watchmaker is already...

t_ch: 真是墙倒众人推…
t_en: People always try to kick you when you're down...

t_ch: 我倒想看看他们…
t_en: But I really wanna see...

t_ch: 要怎么收拾这烂摊子。
t_en: How they are going to clean up this big mess?

t_ch: 垂死挣扎罢了。
t_en: Just their death rattle, that's all.

t_ch: 咱们的经费…
t_en: Our funding...

t_ch: 观众那边的反响…
t_en: The reaction from the audience...

t_ch: 一个外乡人…
t_en: An outworlder...

t_ch: 想重启钟表小子系列…
t_en: I want to reboot the Clockie series...

t_ch: 我看玄。
t_en: I don't have high hopes for that.

t_ch: 终于开始动工了。
t_en: Finally getting started on that job.

t_ch: 有年轻的电影人进来…
t_en: There's a young film worker coming through...

t_ch: 好事，都是好事。
t_en: Good, good. It's all upside.

t_ch: 钟表小子又要开拍了。
t_en: Clockie is going to start shooting again.

t_ch: 还能有以前的味道吗？
t_en: Will it still be the same as before, though?

t_ch: 真期待啊…
t_en: Can't wait to find out...

t_ch: 可以看到…
t_en: We can see...

t_ch: 目前维修工程已经…
t_en: Maintenance work is currently...

t_ch: 客流量也逐渐恢复到…
t_en: Foot traffic has gradually returned to...

t_ch: 车里的内置影院…
t_en: A home theater system built into a car...

t_ch: 我还是喜欢看大屏幕…
t_en: I still prefer seeing things on the big screen...

t_ch: 没办法，还在修嘛。
t_en: Too bad. It's still getting fixed.

t_ch: 家人们，看我身后的…
t_en: Hey, fam, look who's behind me...

t_ch: 要不了多久，就能…
t_en: Just a little while longer, and then...

t_ch: 本期灵异直播…
t_en: On this episode of spooky stories stream...

t_ch: 被神秘力量劈开的…
t_en: Split in two by a mysterious force...

t_ch: 新剧本的超常水准…
t_en: The remarkable quality of the new script...

t_ch: 非常感谢大家的支持。
t_en: Thank you very much for all of your support.

t_ch: 影城的新生时刻！
t_en: The theme park's moment of rebirth!

t_ch: 欢迎收看美梦测评…
t_en: Welcome to Dreamjoy Reviews...

t_ch: 你一定得尝尝这个…
t_en: You simply have to try this...

t_ch: 太难吃了，哈哈。
t_en: Ew, gross! Haha.

t_ch: 我拒绝。
t_en: I refuse.

t_ch: 你也看过那部电影？
t_en: Have you seen that film, too?

t_ch: 居然能遇见同好…
t_en: Never thought I'd meet people into the same stuff...

t_ch: 哈努兄弟简直太酷了…
t_en: Brother Hanu is so cool...

t_ch: 你怎么不说话？
t_en: Say something!

t_ch: 哼。
t_en: Hmph.

t_ch: 太劲爆了！
t_en: I'm fizzing!

t_ch: 我想看的就是这个！
t_en: Now THAT'S what I want to see!

t_ch: 出乎意料的结局…
t_en: Well, that ending was straight out of left field...

t_ch: 笑得肚子有点疼。
t_en: My sides are sore from laughing so much.

t_ch: 不知不觉都这么晚了…
t_en: How did it get so late already...

t_ch: 新剧本的超常水准…
t_en: The remarkable quality of the new script...

t_ch: 非常感谢大家的支持。
t_en: Thank you very much for all of your support.

t_ch: 动画师开始宣传由你参与制作的《美梦往事》外传，引来了游客们的关注……
t_en: The animator begins to promote the Dreamjoy Memoir extra story, in which you are involved, attracting the attention of tourists...

t_ch: 晚上好，亲爱的{NICKNAME}。久疏问候，没想到我们竟能在这片小小梦境中再次相会——
t_en: Good evening, dear {NICKNAME}. I've been meaning to get in touch and never thought that we'd be able to meet up again in a tiny Dreamscape like this...

t_ch: 这是怎样一种缘分啊！
t_en: What a happy twist of fate!

t_ch: 这是怎样一种孽缘…
t_en: What a screwed-up twist of fate...

t_ch: 怎么到处都有你？
t_en: Why do I see you everywhere?

t_ch: 好久不见，乔瓦尼先生。
t_en: Long time no see, Mr. Giovanni.

t_ch: 哈哈，有笑声的地方自然会有{RUBY_B#「愚者」}我{RUBY_E#}的身影。何况我原本就在匹诺康尼投了一个新项目，正巧回来看看成果如何。
t_en: Haha, wherever there is laughter, there {RUBY_B#Fools}I{RUBY_E#} will be. Besides, I invested in a project here in Penacony, so I wanted to check in on how it was doing.

t_ch: ——一款全新的游戏，<color=#dbc291ff>《银河球棒侠传说》</color>！如何，感兴趣吗？
t_en: ...A brand-new game, Legend of the Galactic Baseballer! How about it, are you keen?

t_ch: 仔细说说。
t_en: Tell me more details.

t_ch: 有点可疑…
t_en: Sounds kinda sus...

t_ch: 朋友，谨慎是好事儿——但也可能让你错过许多乐子！
t_en: My friend, caution is a good thing — but it can also make you miss out on a whole lot of fun!

t_ch: 这名号听起来怎么有点耳熟…
t_en: Now why does that name sound familiar...

t_ch: 别着急，我亲爱的朋友，让我慢慢为你介绍。
t_en: Don't worry, my dear friend — I'll fill you in nice and slow.

t_ch: 这是鄙人最近在匹诺康尼赞助的新项目，一款有别于以太战线的游戏。我们利用匹诺康尼特有的梦境技术做了全沉浸式的体验——当然，没朋友也没关系，单人即可游玩！
t_en: This is the new project that I'm sponsoring in Penacony, and it's a different game from Aetherium Wars. We use the unique Dreamscape tech of Penacony to create a completely immersive experience — and of course, you don't even need friends to play it. It's a single-player game!

t_ch: 目前首次测试正在开放中。怎么样，要来试试这次的新玩法吗？
t_en: The open beta is out now. How about it, wanna try out this new gameplay?

t_ch: 晚上好，{M#英俊的先生}{F#美丽的小姐}。久仰星穹列车大名，没想到竟能在这片小小梦境中遇见…
t_en: Good evening, {M#dashing sir}{F#lovely miss}. I've heard so much about the Astral Express but never imagined meeting you in this little Dreamscape here...

t_ch: 请允许我先进行一次自我介绍——鄙人名为乔瓦尼•迪•乔尔吉奥•达•艾普瑟隆，一介来自艾普瑟隆的小小私商。
t_en: Please allow me to introduce myself first: My humble name is Giovanni di Giorgio da Epsilon, and I'm a tiny private trader from Epsilon.

t_ch: 如果我没猜错，你也是来体验<color=#dbc291ff>《银河球棒侠传说》</color>的吧？
t_en: Unless I'm mistaken, you're here to try out Legend of the Galactic Baseballer, right?

t_ch: 《银河球棒侠传说》？
t_en: Legend of the Galactic Baseballer?

t_ch: 没错！
t_en: That's right!

t_ch: 有点可疑…
t_en: Sounds kinda sus...

t_ch: 朋友，谨慎是好事儿——但也可能让你错过许多乐子！
t_en: My friend, caution is a good thing — but it can also make you miss out on a whole lot of fun!

t_ch: 这名号听起来怎么有点耳熟…
t_en: Now why does that name sound familiar...

t_ch: 别着急，我亲爱的朋友，让我慢慢为你介绍。
t_en: Don't worry, my dear friend — I'll fill you in nice and slow.

t_ch: 这是鄙人最近在匹诺康尼赞助的新项目——一款全新的游戏！
t_en: This is the new project that we're sponsoring in Penacony — a brand-new game!

t_ch: 我们利用匹诺康尼特有的梦境技术做了全沉浸式的体验——当然，没朋友也没关系，单人即可游玩！
t_en: We use the unique Dreamscape tech of Penacony to create a completely immersive experience — and of course, you don't even need friends to play it. It's a single-player game!

t_ch: 目前首次测试正在开放中。怎么样，要来体验一下吗？
t_en: The open beta is out now. How about it, wanna try out the experience?

t_ch: 我想玩游戏！
t_en: I'm down to play!

t_ch: 那太好了！目前《银河球棒侠传说》还在研发中，我们需要测试玩家更多的反馈与建议。
t_en: Fantastic! Right now, Legend of the Galactic Baseballer is still under development, and we need more testing to get more user feedback and suggestions.

t_ch: 不，我要来找茬。
t_en: No, I have a bone to pick with you.

t_ch: 有人抄袭我的名字！
t_en: Someone plagiarized my name!

t_ch: 抄就罢了还不给冠名费！
t_en: Using my name is one thing, but not giving me compensation!?

t_ch: 关于这点…无论是艾普瑟隆的版权保护条例、还是匹诺康尼的知识产权登记表中，我们都没有找到关于<color=#dbc291ff>「银河球棒侠」</color>的登记。
t_en: About that... we haven't been able to find any registration for <color=#dbc291ff>Galactic Baseballer</color> in either Epsilon's copyright protection ordinances or in the Penacony registry of IP.

t_ch: 当然，我们也可以把抄袭换个名字——一种伟大的*致敬*。
t_en: Also, we prefer an alternative term: Something like a flattering homage.

t_ch: 如果「银河球棒侠」本人愿意来参与测试的话，鄙人自然会准备一份大礼。
t_en: If the famed Galactic Baseballer is willing to personally participate in the test, I would be delighted to prepare a gift for you.

t_ch: 球棒侠{M#先生}{F#小姐}意下如何？
t_en: What say you, {M#Mister}{F#Miss} Baseballer?

t_ch: 先说来听听。
t_en: I'm all ears.

t_ch: 没问题！请尽管向我提问。想先从哪部分内容听起？
t_en: No problem! Please ask me anything. Which part would you like me to begin with?

t_ch: 游戏要去哪玩？
t_en: Where do I play the game?

t_ch: 这是个好问题！我们曾试着在某位赞助商提供的梦泡上开发，但后来——哦，那枚梦泡里的内容有点太过刺激了，不太适合向大众推广。
t_en: Great question! We tried developing it on a dream bubble supplied by a sponsor, but... *sigh* the content on that dream bubble was a little bit too exciting to be made available to the public.

t_ch: 于是这次我们决定把它做得更*复古*一点儿——请看这边。
t_en: So this time, we decided to make things just a tad bit retro — check this out.

t_ch: 不用注册，上手即玩。这才是游戏最原本的乐趣。
t_en: You can play without registering. That's the fundamental part of the fun when it comes to video games.

t_ch: 这是个什么游戏？
t_en: What kind of game is this?

t_ch: 我想想…宣传词应该是这么说的……
t_en: Let's see... How did we put it in the tagline...

t_ch: 「银河中充满危险，也充满了奇遇。某日，一位小小的勇者踏出家乡，前往征服群星的道路…」
t_en: "The cosmos is full of danger and adventure. One day, a teeny hero left their home world to conquer the stars..."

t_ch: 「挥动球棒，战胜宇宙各处的敌人；寻找宝物，合成星际最强的武器——最终，登上银河球棒侠之巅吧！」
t_en: "Swing your bat and defeat enemies from across the universe. Find treasures and synthesize the most powerful weapons in the cosmos. Then finally, ascend to the pinnacle of the Galactic Baseballer!"

t_ch: 故事向近期在银河间颇有名气的某位球棍使用者*致敬*。取材于现实，再加一点魔幻、一点荒诞…
t_en: The story pays homage to a baseball bat user who recently became famous throughout the cosmos. It's based on reality, with a pinch of fantasy, and just a hint of absurdity thrown in...

t_ch: 我想你会喜欢这个故事的。
t_en: I think you're gonna love this story.

t_ch: 参与测试有报酬吗？
t_en: Is there any remuneration for helping out with testing?

t_ch: 当然！随着游玩进度提升，测试奖励也会逐步解锁。
t_en: Of course! Test rewards will gradually unlock as you progress through the game.

t_ch: ——哦，请别用那种眼神看我。这毕竟是笔买卖，不是慈善。
t_en: ...Oh, please don't look at me like that. It's a business, after all. This isn't a charity.

t_ch: 但请相信我，报酬绝对令你满意。
t_en: But trust me — you're going to be happy with the rewards.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 好了，百闻不如一见。说了这么多，不如亲手体验一把。
t_en: Alright, well, a picture paints a thousand words. I can talk all day, or you can experience it for yourself.

t_ch: 游戏机就在这里。别担心，它会自动保存你的进度，随时回来玩都可以。有问题也可以来问我。
t_en: The game console is right over here. Don't worry, your progress gets auto-saved and you can come back to play any time. Feel free to ask me if you have any questions.

t_ch: 祝你游玩愉快！
t_en: Happy gaming!

t_ch: 嗯？还有什么问题吗？
t_en: Mmm? Any more questions?

t_ch: 游戏要去哪玩？
t_en: Where do I play the game?

t_ch: 这是个好问题！我们曾试着在某位赞助商提供的梦泡上开发，但后来——哦，那枚梦泡里的内容有点太过刺激了，不太适合向大众推广。
t_en: Great question! We tried developing it on a dream bubble supplied by a sponsor, but... *sigh* the content on that dream bubble was a little bit too exciting to be made available to the public.

t_ch: 于是这次我们决定把它做得更*复古*一点儿——请看这边。
t_en: So this time, we decided to make things just a tad bit retro — check this out.

t_ch: 不用注册，上手即玩。这才是游戏最原本的乐趣。
t_en: You can play without registering. That's the fundamental part of the fun when it comes to video games.

t_ch: 这是个什么游戏？
t_en: What kind of game is this?

t_ch: 我想想…宣传词应该是这么说的……
t_en: Let's see... How did we put it in the tagline...

t_ch: 「银河中充满危险，也充满了奇遇。某日，一位小小的勇者踏出家乡，前往征服群星的道路…」
t_en: "The cosmos is full of danger and adventure. One day, a teeny hero left their home world to conquer the stars..."

t_ch: 「挥动球棒，战胜宇宙各处的敌人；寻找宝物，合成星际最强的武器——最终，登上银河球棒侠之巅吧！」
t_en: "Swing your bat and defeat enemies from across the universe. Find treasures and synthesize the most powerful weapons in the cosmos. Then finally, ascend to the pinnacle of the Galactic Baseballer!"

t_ch: 故事向近期在银河间颇有名气的某位球棍使用者*致敬*。取材于现实，再加一点魔幻、一点荒诞…
t_en: The story pays homage to a baseball bat user who recently became famous throughout the cosmos. It's based on reality, with a pinch of fantasy, and just a hint of absurdity thrown in...

t_ch: 我想你会喜欢这个故事的。
t_en: I think you're gonna love this story.

t_ch: 参与测试有报酬吗？
t_en: Is there any remuneration for helping out with testing?

t_ch: 当然！随着游玩进度提升，测试奖励也会逐步解锁。
t_en: Of course! Test rewards will gradually unlock as you progress through the game.

t_ch: ——哦，请别用那种眼神看我。这毕竟是笔买卖，不是慈善。
t_en: ...Oh, please don't look at me like that. It's a business, after all. This isn't a charity.

t_ch: 但请相信我，报酬绝对令你满意。
t_en: But trust me — you're going to be happy with the rewards.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 好了，趁着测试还没结束，赶紧再玩两把吧。
t_en: Alright. Go play a couple more rounds before the testing is over.

t_ch: 祝你游玩愉快！
t_en: Happy gaming!

t_ch: 测试结束，怎么样，体验如何？
t_en: The testing has finished. How'd you find it? How was the experience?

t_ch: 这就结束了？
t_en: That's it?

t_ch: 感觉没玩够…
t_en: I kinda want more...

t_ch: 差评！退钱！
t_en: Terrible! I want a refund!

t_ch: 哈哈，我会如实反馈给制作组的。
t_en: Haha, I'll pass that feedback to the devs exactly as it is.

t_ch: 对首次测试的参与者，我得问几个例行问题——这也是为了收集一些关于游戏体验的反馈。
t_en: I have to ask the participants of the first test a few routine questions — it's also to collect feedback on the game's user experience.

t_ch: 第一个问题：玩得开心吗？
t_en: Question one: Did you have fun?

t_ch: 好玩！还想继续玩！
t_en: It was fun! And I want to play more!

t_ch: 一般，有点困。
t_en: It's mid, and a little uninspired.

t_ch: 不如垃圾桶有趣。
t_en: Trashcans are more interesting than this.

t_ch: 接下来是第二个问题：作为玩家，你愿意为这款游戏掏腰包吗？
t_en: Next up is question two: As a player, would you be willing to pay for this game?

t_ch: 我狂买！
t_en: I'd snatch it up!

t_ch: 没钱了…
t_en: I don't have any money...

t_ch: 没关系，我会打劫。
t_en: No worries. I'll get the money. Somehow.

t_ch: 嗯…原来是这样。
t_en: Oh... is that so?

t_ch: 还差最后一个问题，我很好奇你的答案——
t_en: And the final question. I'm really curious about your answer to this one:

t_ch: 选择黄金球棒，成为寰宇第一侠…
t_en: Select the golden bat and become the top baseballer in the universe...

t_ch: 你描述了一个振奋人心的励志故事。
t_en: You tell an inspiring story of aspiration.

t_ch: 「黄金球棒赐予你无与伦比的强大力量，宇宙间无人不知你银河球棒侠的美名。球棒侠成为星际间最炙手可热的形象，故乡的星球为你立起巨大的雕塑，也撬动了无数游客的钱袋。但由于你没有登记版权，只能眼睁睁看着金币溜入其他人的口袋…」
t_en: "The golden bat bestows unto you unparalleled powers, and everyone in the universe comes to know the name of the Galactic Baseballer, which becomes the most unrivaled figure across the universe. Your home planet erects a huge sculpture in your name as it pries money out of eager tourists' wallets. However, since you failed to register the copyright, you can only watch as the coins flow away into other people's pockets..."

t_ch: （…总感觉变成可悲的现实故事了。）
t_en: (...Sounds like this turned into a realistic tragedy somewhere along the way.)

t_ch: 结合「球棒侠」本人的遭遇来看…真是充满教育意义的结局。哈哈，我会考虑采纳的。
t_en: Combined with what happens to the Galactic Baseballer... it's a real educational ending. Haha, I'll add this outcome into consideration.

t_ch: 选择黑暗球棒，成为银河大反派…
t_en: Pick the dark bat and become the greatest villain in all the cosmos...

t_ch: 你描述了一个令人深思的寓言故事。
t_en: You tell a thought-provoking fable.

t_ch: 「屠龙者终将成龙，沾染禁忌力量的球棒侠也是如此。等到幡然醒悟时，一切早已无法悔改。你遮住自己的面容，将自己与黑暗球棒一同封印在银河的某个角落，等待下一个被欲望侵染的可怜虫出现…」
t_en: "As the dragonslayer eventually devolves into a dragon, so too does the Galactic Baseballer succumb when they touch upon the forbidden powers. By the time you realize what is happening, there is no longer any path to forgiveness. You cover up your face and seal yourself away in a dark corner of the universe with your dark bat, waiting for the next piteous adventurer who is driven mad by desire to turn up..."

t_ch: （…但真有玩家会选一看就是错误选项的「黑暗球棒」吗？）
t_en: (...But would any players actually choose the obviously wrong "dark bat" as an option?)

t_ch: 嗯…很有意思的结局，我会考虑采纳的。
t_en: Hmm... what a fascinating ending. I'll consider it.

t_ch: 选择自己的球棒，成为热心好人…
t_en: Pick your own bat, and become a righteous person...

t_ch: 你描述了一个平平淡淡的暖心故事。
t_en: You tell an ordinary but heartwarming story.

t_ch: 「你放弃了诱惑，拿起了自己最熟悉的那只球棒。棍身已经磨损折旧，但却是手中最真实的力量。你没有成为银河第一的球棒侠。但每当他人落难时，都会有位使用球棒的好心人赶来助上一臂之力…」
t_en: "Giving up all temptation, you pick up the bat you're most familiar with. It's worn and dull, but it holds the truest powers when it rests in your hands. You have not become the ultimate baseballer in the cosmos, but whenever trouble befalls anyone, there will always be a kind-hearted individual arriving to help out with this bat in hand..."

t_ch: （…银河好人，或许平平淡淡才是真。）
t_en: (...A cosmic altruist. Maybe it's for the best if they're someone ordinary.)

t_ch: 嗯…别出新意的结局，我会考虑采纳的。
t_en: Mmm... a rather innovative ending, and I'll consider using it.

t_ch: 弃武从文，成为银河笔杆子侠！
t_en: Spurn arms and take up the pen: Become the literary hero of the cosmos!

t_ch: 你描述了一个另辟蹊径的奇妙故事。
t_en: You tell a marvelous story about finding another way.

t_ch: 「你看着眼前的三只球棒，一个念头闪电般刺入你的脑海：『当球棒侠救不了所有人！ 』为了拯救宇宙的蒙昧无知，你决定扔掉武器，奔赴新的战场——成为银河笔杆子侠！」
t_en: "As you gaze at the three bats in front of you, a thought flashes into your skull like lightning: The Galactic Baseballer cannot save everyone! To save the universe from its own ignorance, you decide to throw down your weapon and set foot on a new battlefield: That of the cosmos's literary hero!"

t_ch: （…说起来，浣熊也能写字吗？）
t_en: (...Speaking of which, can raccoons even write?)

t_ch: 哈哈，很好！非常「欢愉」！结局就选它了！
t_en: Haha, fantastic! How "Elating"! That's my ending!

t_ch: 我最开始就不会这么设计。
t_en: I wouldn't design it like this at the start.

t_ch: 哈哈，非常有建设性的意见！我会考虑采纳的。
t_en: Haha, very constructive criticism! I'll consider it.

t_ch: 时间不早了，我得把这些反馈带回去，让那些开发人员看着改改。
t_en: It's getting late and I have to get this feedback back to the developers for them to run their changes.

t_ch: 真希望他们能在验收前改完。要知道，其他几个赞助商可没我这么好说话。
t_en: I hope they can get things done before project delivery. Y'know, the other sponsors aren't nearly as nice as I am.

t_ch: 在临走前——这个请收好，专门为「银河球棒侠」准备的额外大礼包！
t_en: Before you go — take this. It's a bonus present especially for the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: 这也太「欢愉」了…
t_en: Oh wow, I'm sooo "Elated" now...

t_ch: 哈哈，感谢你的赞美。这正是对{RUBY_B#「愚者」}我们{RUBY_E#}最好的褒奖。
t_en: Haha, thanks for the compliment. That's the best praise ever for {RUBY_B#Fools}us{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 游戏机我留在这，测试结束前都可以随意游玩。
t_en: I'll leave the gaming console here, and you can keep playing it any time until testing finishes.

t_ch: 那么，请允许我先行一步——下次有乐子的地方再会！
t_en: So, if I could be so bold as to take off — and may we meet again some place fun!

t_ch: 没错！
t_en: That's right!

t_ch: 后面的游戏内容还在开发中，预计下一次测试时上线。
t_en: The upcoming content for the game is still in development and is expected to go online by the next test.

t_ch: 如果说在故事的结尾，小浣熊跳入黑洞，战胜宇宙间所有的敌人，终于获得了选择自己梦想中最强球棒的机会…
t_en: If, at the end of the story, the little raccoon gets to jump into the black hole, defeats all its enemies in the universe, and finally gets the chance to choose the ultimate bat of its dreams...

t_ch: 代表传奇的黄金球棒、被禁忌封印的黑暗球棒…你认为，「球棒侠」会如何选择呢？
t_en: The legendary golden bat, or the forbidden dark bat... which bat do you think the baseballer would choose?

t_ch: 听说你有事找我？
t_en: I heard you are looking for me?

t_ch: 对，这件事闹腾得挺厉害的，算是「公司」的机密吧…就连温世齐大哥也不敢张扬，他让我私下找你，切莫声张。
t_en: Yes, this is all rather confounding and one of the IPC's top secrets... Not even Wen Shiqi dared spread the word. He asked me to find you in private and to keep things quiet.

t_ch: 这件事…温世齐大哥说，整个空间站里的科员跟你没法比，无论是人脉、身手还是人品，你都是最佳人选，所以他让我把你喊过来……
t_en: So, with this business... Wen Shiqi said that none of the researchers aboard the space station can hold a candle to you, be it your connections, your skills, or your character — you're the top candidate for all of them. So he asked me to call you over...

t_ch: 那么究竟是什么事呢？
t_en: So what exactly is going on?

t_ch: 简单来说，外面各方人马都发布了一些委托，是捞油水的大好机会，大哥他觉得你挺合适的，想把你内推到公司那儿。
t_en: Put simply, every man and his dog out there have issued assignments, and they're a great way to make some money. Wen Shiqi thinks you'd be suitable, and wants to do an internal recommendation for you to the IPC.

t_ch: 泄露机密，你就不怕出事吗？
t_en: Are you sure it's okay to leak secrets just like this?

t_ch: 机密嘛，不被泄漏的算什么「机密」呢？
t_en: What's the point of a secret if it never gets leaked?

t_ch: 再说了，我又不是「公司」的人，空间站可是黑塔女士罩着的呢，就算她老人家不在线，天花板塌了也有艾丝妲站长顶着嘛。
t_en: And besides, I'm not in the IPC. The space station comes under the auspices of Madam Herta and even if she wasn't online, Lead Researcher Asta is there to keep the sky from falling.

t_ch: 其实吧，就是有个叫波提欧的「星际牛仔」，最近干了几票大的，闹得人仰马翻的，连带我们空间站也倒了霉。
t_en: Actually, there's this "interstellar cowboy" called Boothill who's recently done a few big jobs and really caused a lot of trouble. He even got one over on our space station.

t_ch: 我听说，黑塔女士和某几个科室授权给「公司」研发的产品，在运输途中被劫，现在下落不明，害得大家又得返工……
t_en: I heard Madam Herta and a few of the departments commissioned the IPC to develop some products, but these items were robbed while being transported. Now their whereabouts are unknown, and everyone has to re-do all their work...

t_ch: 「公司」那边也很头疼，正准备通缉这家伙呢，各路人马也纷纷砸钱砸资源地想把他挖出来。
t_en: The IPC are in a pickle too. They're preparing a wanted bounty on this guy, and lots of other people are throwing out all kinds of bounties and offers to try and hunt him down too.

t_ch: 「嘿嘿…情况越混乱，咱们就越是有机会从中捞点油水」——咳咳，这是温世齐大哥的原话，他说有好事就应该是第一时间跟好朋友分享，所以把你喊过来了。
t_en: "Hehe... The more chaotic a situation is, the more money we stand to make from it"... Ahem, that's what Wen Shiqi said verbatim. He told me that good news should be shared in the first instance with good friends — and so he called you over.

t_ch: 那我该怎么做呢？
t_en: So what should I do, then?

t_ch: 不愧是好兄弟，请指导我！
t_en: What a true friend. Please, give me pointers!

t_ch: 稍等，你的信息我已经内推给「公司」那边了，这次的专项委托是「公司」发起的，要等他们核实下你的身份——噢，{NICKNAME}，你可真了不得， 那边秒批了。
t_en: Hang on, I've already passed on your info to the IPC. Today's special assignment was issued by the IPC, so we have to wait for them to verify your identity... Oh, {NICKNAME} — you're incredible. They approved it in a heartbeat.

t_ch: 好了，具体的内容已经发给你了，你自己看着办吧，不过你得注意了——捞好处可以，但要注意安全，那个牛仔的手段听说挺毒辣的。
t_en: Good, specific details have been sent to you, and you can do it however you see fit. Remember — you can definitely turn a profit on it, but you need to be very careful. I heard that cowboy can be positively diabolical.

t_ch: 嘘，「专项委托」的事你可别对外声张——咱们得闷声发财，注意安全。
t_en: Shhh, don't leak anything about our "special assignment" — we gotta keep quiet, get rich, and stay safe.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}，你终于来了。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, you've finally arrived.

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 礼品在哪里？
t_en: Where is the gift?

t_ch: 请先别急，听我慢慢道来。
t_en: Patience, please. I'll explain everything in detail.

t_ch: 事情是这样的——为了重现你在比赛中的英姿，我们向闻名宇宙的大艺术家定制了一部特殊影片。
t_en: Here's what happened — To recreate your heroic performance in the competition, we have asked a cosmically-renowned artist to make a film dedicated to your name.

t_ch: 就像梦泡一样，这部影片储存着热砂盛典相关的「记忆」。但在对这部进行安全检查的时候，一位员工消失在了影片之中，与我们失去了联系……
t_en: Just like a dream bubble, this film stores the memories related to the Scorchsand Festival. But during the review process, a staff member got lost in the film...

t_ch: 闹鬼了？
t_en: Oh, no! Spooky!

t_ch: 谁说不是呢，梦里的怪事可太多了。
t_en: Ain't that right? So many shenanigans in dreams.

t_ch: 是恶作剧吗？
t_en: Is this a prank?

t_ch: 也许是，听说那位艺术家的性情颇为古怪，搞不好在作品中动了什么手脚……
t_en: Maybe, but from what I've heard, that artist is quite the eccentric character. Perhaps they embedded some shenanigans in the dream.

t_ch: 擅自翻阅他人物品的惩罚？
t_en: Serves you right. You lot didn't ask my consent first for all this.

t_ch: 这…我们也是为了你的安全着想。自从会场差点被一个包裹炸成废墟之后，家族规定我们必须对入梦物品进行检查，请见谅。
t_en: Well... It was all precautionary measures taken for your personal safety. Ever since that one time when the arena almost got blown up to smithereens by a suspicious package, The Family has explicitly demanded all items to be thoroughly inspected before they are allowed entry into the dreamscape. Please understand.

t_ch: 这部影片是为你制作的，可能也只有你可以救出那位员工了……
t_en: This film is dedicated to you. Perhaps only you can save that staff member...

t_ch: 那盘录像带就在沙发上，我们的盛会巨星，交给你了！
t_en: That film tape is on the sofa. O, our heroic Festive Superstar, we are all counting on you.

t_ch: 这部影片是为你制作的，或许也只有你可以解开谜题，救出那位员工了。
t_en: This film is dedicated to you. Perhaps only you are capable of solving this mystery and saving that staff member.

t_ch: 那盘录像带就在沙发上，麻烦你了，我们的盛会巨星！
t_en: That tape is on the sofa. O, our heroic Festive Superstar, please save us.

t_ch: 一卷散发金属光泽的录像带，封面刻着古怪的图案，像是一只两栖动物的眼睛——它正在注视着你。
t_en: A gleaming tape, metallic in its luster, adorned with a peculiar design on its cover — like some kind of amphibian's eye, fixated upon you.
